questions.title: Print All Binary Strings of Length n
questions.prompt:
Given an integer n, print all binary strings of length n.

A binary string contains only 0 and 1.

Return the strings in a list instead of printing them.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Binary Strings"><defs><linearGradient id="g" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff7ed"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ffd7a1"/></linearGradient></defs><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="url(#g)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.16)"/><text x="34" y="48" font-size="26" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Binary Strings</text><text x="34" y="82" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">At every position, choose 0 or 1</text><g transform="translate(74,122)"><rect x="0" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="105" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">choose</text><path d="M 220 37 L 310 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 296 25 L 310 37 L 296 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="330" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="435" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">explore</text><path d="M 550 37 L 640 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 626 25 L 640 37 L 626 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="660" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="765" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">undo</text></g><text x="490" y="244" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">n positions create 2^n answers</text><text x="34" y="286" font-size="16" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Choice • Explore • Undo</text></svg>
hints: Build the answer one character at a time.
At each step, try '0' and then try '1'.
When the path length becomes n, save it.
Backtracking means return after exploring a choice.
questions.starter_code:
def binary_strings(n):
    result = []

    def backtrack(path):
        # TODO
        pass

    backtrack("")
    return result

print(binary_strings(2))
print(binary_strings(3))
questions.solution_code:
def binary_strings(n):
    result = []

    def backtrack(path):
        if len(path) == n:
            result.append(path)
            return

        backtrack(path + "0")
        backtrack(path + "1")

    backtrack("")
    return result

print(binary_strings(2))
print(binary_strings(3))

questions.title: Generate All Subsets of a List
questions.prompt:
Given a list of distinct numbers, return all possible subsets.

The empty subset must also be included.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Subsets"><defs><linearGradient id="g" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff7ed"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ffd7a1"/></linearGradient></defs><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="url(#g)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.16)"/><text x="34" y="48" font-size="26" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Subsets</text><text x="34" y="82" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">For every number: take it or skip it</text><g transform="translate(74,122)"><rect x="0" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="105" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">choose</text><path d="M 220 37 L 310 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 296 25 L 310 37 L 296 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="330" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="435" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">explore</text><path d="M 550 37 L 640 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 626 25 L 640 37 L 626 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="660" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="765" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">undo</text></g><text x="490" y="244" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">two choices for each item</text><text x="34" y="286" font-size="16" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Choice • Explore • Undo</text></svg>
hints: Use an index to know which number you are deciding about.
For each number, make two calls: skip and take.
When index reaches len(nums), save a copy of path.
Use path.append and path.pop for choose and undo.
questions.starter_code:
def subsets(nums):
    result = []

    def backtrack(index, path):
        # TODO
        pass

    backtrack(0, [])
    return result

print(subsets([1, 2, 3]))
questions.solution_code:
def subsets(nums):
    result = []

    def backtrack(index, path):
        if index == len(nums):
            result.append(path[:])
            return

        backtrack(index + 1, path)

        path.append(nums[index])
        backtrack(index + 1, path)
        path.pop()

    backtrack(0, [])
    return result

print(subsets([1, 2, 3]))

questions.title: Generate Valid Parentheses
questions.prompt:
Given n pairs of parentheses, return all valid parentheses strings.

A valid string never closes before it opens.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Valid Parentheses"><defs><linearGradient id="g" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff7ed"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ffd7a1"/></linearGradient></defs><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="url(#g)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.16)"/><text x="34" y="48" font-size="26" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Valid Parentheses</text><text x="34" y="82" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Open if possible, close only if safe</text><g transform="translate(74,122)"><rect x="0" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="105" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">choose</text><path d="M 220 37 L 310 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 296 25 L 310 37 L 296 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="330" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="435" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">explore</text><path d="M 550 37 L 640 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 626 25 L 640 37 L 626 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="660" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="765" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">undo</text></g><text x="490" y="244" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">never let close count exceed open count</text><text x="34" y="286" font-size="16" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Choice • Explore • Undo</text></svg>

You may add ')' if close_count < open_count.

questions.title: Combination Sum
questions.prompt:
Given a list of positive integers candidates and a target, return all combinations whose sum equals target.

You may use the same candidate more than once.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Combination Sum"><defs><linearGradient id="g" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff7ed"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ffd7a1"/></linearGradient></defs><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="url(#g)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.16)"/><text x="34" y="48" font-size="26" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Combination Sum</text><text x="34" y="82" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Keep choosing while remaining target is not negative</text><g transform="translate(74,122)"><rect x="0" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="105" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">choose</text><path d="M 220 37 L 310 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 296 25 L 310 37 L 296 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="330" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="435" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">explore</text><path d="M 550 37 L 640 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 626 25 L 640 37 L 626 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="660" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="765" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">undo</text></g><text x="490" y="244" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">same number can be reused</text><text x="34" y="286" font-size="16" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Choice • Explore • Undo</text></svg>

questions.title: All Paths in a Maze
questions.prompt:
Given a grid where 0 means open and 1 means blocked, find all paths from top-left to bottom-right.

You may move only Down or Right.

Return paths as strings using D and R.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Maze Paths"><defs><linearGradient id="g" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff7ed"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ffd7a1"/></linearGradient></defs><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="url(#g)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.16)"/><text x="34" y="48" font-size="26" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Maze Paths</text><text x="34" y="82" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Move through open cells only</text><g transform="translate(74,122)"><rect x="0" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="105" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">choose</text><path d="M 220 37 L 310 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 296 25 L 310 37 L 296 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="330" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="435" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">explore</text><path d="M 550 37 L 640 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 626 25 L 640 37 L 626 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="660" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="765" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">undo</text></g><text x="490" y="244" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">D and R build the path</text><text x="34" y="286" font-size="16" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Choice • Explore • Undo</text></svg>
hints: Start from cell (0, 0).
If the current cell is outside the grid or blocked, return.
If you reach the bottom-right cell, save the path.
Try Down and Right recursively.
questions.starter_code:
def maze_paths(maze):
    result = []
    rows = len(maze)
    cols = len(maze[0])

    def backtrack(r, c, path):
        # TODO
        pass

    backtrack(0, 0, "")
    return result

maze = [[0,0,0],[0,1,0],[0,0,0]]
print(maze_paths(maze))
questions.solution_code:
def maze_paths(maze):
    result = []
    rows = len(maze)
    cols = len(maze[0])

    def backtrack(r, c, path):
        if r < 0 or c < 0 or r >= rows or c >= cols:
            return
        if maze[r][c] == 1:
            return
        if r == rows - 1 and c == cols - 1:
            result.append(path)
            return

        backtrack(r + 1, c, path + "D")
        backtrack(r, c + 1, path + "R")

    backtrack(0, 0, "")
    return result

maze = [[0,0,0],[0,1,0],[0,0,0]]
print(maze_paths(maze))

questions.title: Rat in a Maze with Four Directions
questions.prompt:
Given a square maze where 1 means open and 0 means blocked, find all paths from top-left to bottom-right.

The rat can move Down, Left, Right, and Up.

Return paths as strings using D, L, R, and U.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Rat in a Maze"><defs><linearGradient id="g" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff7ed"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ffd7a1"/></linearGradient></defs><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="url(#g)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.16)"/><text x="34" y="48" font-size="26" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Rat in a Maze</text><text x="34" y="82" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Use visited cells to avoid cycles</text><g transform="translate(74,122)"><rect x="0" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="105" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">choose</text><path d="M 220 37 L 310 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 296 25 L 310 37 L 296 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="330" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="435" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">explore</text><path d="M 550 37 L 640 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 626 25 L 640 37 L 626 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="660" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="765" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">undo</text></g><text x="490" y="244" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">D, L, R, U search</text><text x="34" y="286" font-size="16" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Choice • Explore • Undo</text></svg>
hints: Four-direction movement can create cycles.
Use a visited matrix.
Mark a cell visited before exploring neighbors.
Unmark it when returning from recursion.
questions.starter_code:
def rat_in_maze(maze):
    n = len(maze)
    result = []
    visited = [[False] * n for _ in range(n)]

    def backtrack(r, c, path):
        # TODO
        pass

    backtrack(0, 0, "")
    return result

maze = [[1,0,0,0],[1,1,0,1],[1,1,0,0],[0,1,1,1]]
print(rat_in_maze(maze))
questions.solution_code:
def rat_in_maze(maze):
    n = len(maze)
    result = []
    visited = [[False] * n for _ in range(n)]
    moves = [(1, 0, "D"), (0, -1, "L"), (0, 1, "R"), (-1, 0, "U")]

    def backtrack(r, c, path):
        if r < 0 or c < 0 or r >= n or c >= n:
            return
        if maze[r][c] == 0 or visited[r][c]:
            return
        if r == n - 1 and c == n - 1:
            result.append(path)
            return

        visited[r][c] = True
        for dr, dc, letter in moves:
            backtrack(r + dr, c + dc, path + letter)
        visited[r][c] = False

    backtrack(0, 0, "")
    return result

maze = [[1,0,0,0],[1,1,0,1],[1,1,0,0],[0,1,1,1]]
print(rat_in_maze(maze))

questions.title: Word Search in a Grid
questions.prompt:
Given a grid of letters and a word, return True if the word exists in the grid.

The word can be built from horizontally or vertically adjacent cells.

The same cell cannot be used twice in one word path.

hints: Try starting from every cell.
Use index to track which character of the word is being matched.
Return True when index reaches len(word).
Temporarily mark the current cell as visited, then undo it.

questions.title: Solve the N-Queens Problem
questions.prompt:
Place n queens on an n x n chessboard so that no two queens attack each other.

Return all valid boards using 'Q' for queen and '.' for empty cell.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="N-Queens"><defs><linearGradient id="g" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff7ed"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ffd7a1"/></linearGradient></defs><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="url(#g)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.16)"/><text x="34" y="48" font-size="26" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">N-Queens</text><text x="34" y="82" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">One queen per row, safe column and diagonals</text><g transform="translate(74,122)"><rect x="0" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="105" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">choose</text><path d="M 220 37 L 310 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 296 25 L 310 37 L 296 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="330" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="435" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">explore</text><path d="M 550 37 L 640 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 626 25 L 640 37 L 626 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="660" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="765" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">undo</text></g><text x="490" y="244" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">constraints cut bad branches early</text><text x="34" y="286" font-size="16" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Choice • Explore • Undo</text></svg>
hints: Place one queen row by row.
Track used columns.
Track two diagonals: row - col and row + col.
When row equals n, save the board.

questions.solution_code:
def solve_n_queens(n):
    result = []
    board = [["."] * n for _ in range(n)]
    cols = set()
    diag1 = set()
    diag2 = set()

    def backtrack(row):
        if row == n:
            result.append(["".join(r) for r in board])
            return

        for col in range(n):
            if col in cols or (row - col) in diag1 or (row + col) in diag2:
                continue

            board[row][col] = "Q"
            cols.add(col)
            diag1.add(row - col)
            diag2.add(row + col)

            backtrack(row + 1)

            board[row][col] = "."
            cols.remove(col)
            diag1.remove(row - col)
            diag2.remove(row + col)

    backtrack(0)
    return result

print(solve_n_queens(4))

questions.title: Sudoku Solver
questions.prompt:
Given a 9 x 9 Sudoku board, fill the empty cells.

Empty cells are represented by '.'.

Modify the board in-place and return it.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Sudoku Solver"><defs><linearGradient id="g" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff7ed"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ffd7a1"/></linearGradient></defs><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="url(#g)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.16)"/><text x="34" y="48" font-size="26" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Sudoku Solver</text><text x="34" y="82" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Try digits only when row, column, and box allow them</text><g transform="translate(74,122)"><rect x="0" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="105" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">choose</text><path d="M 220 37 L 310 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 296 25 L 310 37 L 296 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="330" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="435" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">explore</text><path d="M 550 37 L 640 37" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 626 25 L 640 37 L 626 49" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="660" y="0" width="210" height="74" rx="18" fill="white" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="765" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">undo</text></g><text x="490" y="244" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">backtrack when a digit fails later</text><text x="34" y="286" font-size="16" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Choice • Explore • Undo</text></svg>
hints: Find the next empty cell.
Try digits from '1' to '9'.
A digit is valid if it is absent from the row, column, and 3x3 box.
Place, recurse, and undo if the choice fails.
